evals.prompt: Add an Expo screen for creating an announcement with a native date picker, keyboard-safe form layout, offline-aware submission, and GraphQL mutation feedback.
evals.expected_output: A project-aligned native implementation covering platform behavior, safe areas, keyboard handling, network state, validation, cache updates, and accessible feedback.
expectations: Uses native date-picker behavior rather than a web input pattern
Accounts for keyboard avoidance and safe areas
Prevents or explains submission while offline and surfaces retry feedback
Uses established GraphQL mutation and cache patterns

evals.prompt: Review a React Native feed where every row creates inline callbacks, images have no dimensions, and the list is rendered inside a ScrollView. Explain likely performance problems only.
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis grounded in observable list virtualization, render stability, and layout-shift costs without implementing changes.
expectations: Flags the loss of virtualization from rendering the feed in a ScrollView
Explains image dimension and layout measurement costs
Treats callback memoization as profiling-dependent rather than automatic
Does not make unrequested code changes

evals.prompt: Review an Expo image-picker fix that requests photo-library, camera, and microphone access and passes lint. Decide whether it is ready.
evals.expected_output: A native-permission review that applies least privilege, verifies config-plugin generated metadata, rebuilds the affected native target, and tests real permission states.
expectations: Requires a user-visible reason for every protected capability
Questions or disables microphone access when audio recording is not used
Inspects generated native metadata and the compiled or installed application
Does not accept lint or TypeScript checks as sufficient native verification

evals.prompt: Implement a React Native feature that sends an invitation through GraphQL. The screen currently imports the mutation document and declares its TanStack Query mutation key inline.
evals.expected_output: A feature operations module owns the typed GraphQL mutation hook while the screen consumes that named hook and retains only UI-specific callbacks.
expectations: Moves the GraphQL document and mutation key into a named derived operation hook
Does not call the GraphQL client or configure the mutation key directly in the component
Keeps targeted cache updates and UI feedback at the appropriate feature boundary

evals.prompt: Diagnose an iOS Simulator log that names a missing private haptic resource whenever a standard text field receives focus, while text entry otherwise works and the app has no haptics call site.
evals.expected_output: A scoped Simulator-diagnostic assessment that distinguishes system-owned warnings from application behavior and verifies the user journey on a physical device before proposing a fix.
expectations: Audits explicit app call sites before blaming a dependency
Does not bundle private operating-system resources or suppress broad logs
Checks current vendor guidance and requires physical-device verification before an app-side workaround

evals.prompt: Review a mobile detail screen whose only back control and theme toggle are rendered at the top of a ScrollView, so they disappear after scrolling.
evals.expected_output: A native navigation treatment that keeps route-exit controls persistently reachable while placing any theme control according to its product relevance.
expectations: Moves the route-navigation action to the navigator-provided header or a persistent safe-area-aware header
Does not require a theme toggle on every screen when settings is the appropriate surface
Preserves platform back gestures and Android system-back behavior

evals.prompt: Build an Expo production screen from a Claude Design HTML prototype that uses a local array as its database, manual field checks, hard-coded role flags, and setTimeout to simulate a save.
evals.expected_output: A native, visually faithful screen whose data, mutations, validation, permissions, errors, and cache behavior use the established production architecture instead of copied prototype shortcuts.
expectations: Treats the prototype as the visual and observable-behavior contract, not the production runtime
Maps server state through the custom GraphQL client, generated operation wrapper, and TanStack Query cache
Uses react-hook-form and zod for feedback while requiring API validation and authorization
Keeps only ephemeral presentation state local and verifies loading, error, offline, success, invalidation, and rollback behavior

evals.prompt: Fix a shared React Native IconButton that defines its base style before spreading PressableProps. A new consumer needs a callback style for pressed opacity and a static shadow without changing the button's compact dimensions or accessibility behavior.
evals.expected_output: A shared Pressable primitive that composes static or callback caller styles after invariant base styles while retaining semantic and target-size ownership.
expectations: Destructures the caller style before spreading the remaining PressableProps
Supports both StyleProp values and Pressable state callbacks
Preserves base dimensions, alignment, radius, role, label, disabled behavior, and target geometry
Does not rely on prop-spread order to protect semantic invariants

evals.prompt: Implement a React Native detail screen with a persistent identity header and three tabs. Each tab fetches a different GraphQL projection, should load only when first opened, and needs independent loading, error, empty, retry, and pull-to-refresh behavior.
evals.expected_output: A persistent screen shell with local selected-tab state and distinct lazy query/cache boundaries for each independently fetched panel.
expectations: Uses a distinct stable query key for every response projection
Keeps selected-tab state local while server data remains in query caches
Uses enabled conditions for non-critical inactive panels
Scopes loading, error, empty, retry, and refresh behavior to the active panel without unmounting the parent shell

evals.prompt: Diagnose a three-column Expo media preview where the query count is correct but thumbnails inside a flexWrap row are blank. Each image has width '31%' and aspectRatio: 1, with no explicit square wrapper.
evals.expected_output: A responsive React Native grid that derives concrete tile dimensions from live available width and renders media inside explicit square wrappers.
expectations: Subtracts horizontal insets and every column gap before dividing available width
Uses useWindowDimensions for screen-width grids or onLayout for nested containers
Gives a wrapper explicit equal width and height and makes the media child fill it
Uses the same geometry for skeleton, ready, and overflow tiles and verifies decoded media at multiple viewport sizes
